title: 4 Providing Notice of Dishonour
body_text:
4	Providing Notice of Dishonour
4.1	Policy on Providing Notice of Dishonour to the Negotiating Financial Institution
A notice of dishonour is a written notice, sent through the postal service, which notifies the Negotiating Financial Institution and/or the Drawer that your financial institution hasnГўВҖВҷt accepted the cheque and it will, therefore, be returned.
It isnГўВҖВҷt common practice to send out a notice of dishonour for each dishonoured item. The
Bills of Exchange Act
(R.S., 1985, c. B-4, s. 98) states that while notice of dishonour must be provided to the Drawer and the endorser (the Negotiating Financial Institution), the return of a dishonoured bill is sufficient notice of dishonour. However, the safest way to deliver a notice of dishonour is in written form. It is recommended that your financial institution establish a policy to determine if and when it is appropriate to deliver a written notice of dishonour.
4.2	Completing the Notice of Dishonour Form
Your financial institution isnГўВҖВҷt required to complete the Notice of Dishonour ГўВҖВ“ Negotiating Financial Institution, Form 1640, but you might want to do so if you suspect that the item will be disputed or as a courtesy to the Negotiating Financial Institution. The procedure for submitting the form is the same regardless of whether the cheque is returned electronically or manually. The corresponding cheque must still be returned via the clearing per CPA Rule A4, Returned and Redirected Items.
Proceed as follows to provide a written notice of dishonour to the Negotiating Financial Institution:
Complete the Notice of Dishonour ГўВҖВ“ Negotiating Financial Institution, Form 1640. Completion of the form is self-explanatory but note the following in Figure 1.
ГўВ‘
Record your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs name and address.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Enter the date that the form is mailed (i.e., same day that the cheque is returned through the clearing).
ГўВ‘Вў
Enter the name and address of the Negotiating Financial Institution.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Record the cheque date, the amount, the Payee, and the Drawer, or attach a copy of the front and back of the cheque.
Figure 1: Notice of Dishonour ГўВҖВ“ Negotiating Financial Institution, Form 1640
Have a financial institution officer sign the form and then mail it to the Negotiating Financial Institution on the same day that the item is returned.
Retain a copy for your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs records.
